mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening supply chain that boosts corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the 2020 COVID-19 supply shock.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the 2020 COVID-19 labor market shock.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts signals a strengthening demand for residential construction, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the 2020 COVID-19 housing market shock.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the 2020 COVID-19 demand shock.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in tightening monetary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the 2018 monetary policy shock.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain that boosts corporate risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in weakening supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the 2020 COVID-19 supply shock.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment signals a strengthening labor market that boosts wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening labor periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the 2020 COVID-19 labor market shock.
